{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QV7SWJaRHeSzdJN6QG9zP6","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→#!/usr/bin/env python3\n     2→\"\"\"\n     3→Terminal-Bench Claude Code Hooks (unified script).\n     4→\n     5→Handles all three hook events via stdin payload inspection:\n     6→- PreToolUse: tracks state, always allows\n     7→- PostToolUse: tracks errors, verification results, loop detection\n     8→- Stop: blocks exit if verification hasn't been attempted\n     9→\n    10→Protocol:\n    11→  Input: JSON via stdin\n    12→  Output: JSON via stdout wrapped in {\"hookSpecificOutput\": {...}}\n    13→\n    14→State persisted in /tmp/.tbench_state.json across invocations.\n    15→\"\"\"\n    16→\n    17→from __future__ import annotations\n    18→\n    19→import json\n    20→import sys\n    21→import time\n    22→from pathlib import Path\n    23→\n    24→STATE_FILE = Path(\"/tmp/.tbench_state.json\")\n    25→\n    26→# Patterns that indicate the agent is running verification tests\n    27→VERIFICATION_PATTERNS = [\n    28→    \"tests/test.sh\",\n    29→    \"tests/test_\",\n    30→    \"pytest tests/\",\n    31→    \"pytest -v\",\n    32→    \"python -m pytest\",\n    33→    \"python3 -m pytest\",\n    34→    \"make test\",\n    35→    \"cargo test\",\n    36→    \"npm test\",\n    37→    \"go test\",\n    38→    \"bash test\",\n    39→    \"./test.sh\",\n    40→    \"ctest\",\n    41→]\n    42→\n    43→# Patterns that indicate test success\n    44→SUCCESS_PATTERNS = [\n    45→    \"passed\",\n    46→    \" ok\",\n    47→    \"0 failed\",\n    48→    \"tests passed\",\n    49→    \"all tests\",\n    50→    \"PASS\",\n    51→    \"OK (\",\n    52→    \"0 errors\",\n    53→    \"Build succeeded\",\n    54→    \"All checks passed\",\n    55→]\n    56→\n    57→# Patterns that indicate errors in command output\n    58→ERROR_PATTERNS = [\n    59→    \"error:\",\n    60→    \"Error:\",\n    61→    \"ERROR:\",\n    62→    \"traceback\",\n    63→    \"Traceback\",\n    64→    \"FAILED\",\n    65→    \"not found\",\n    66→    \"No such file\",\n    67→    \"permission denied\",\n    68→    \"Permission denied\",\n    69→    \"command not found\",\n    70→    \"ModuleNotFoundError\",\n    71→    \"ImportError\",\n    72→    \"SyntaxError\",\n    73→    \"NameError\",\n    74→    \"TypeError\",\n    75→    \"ValueError\",\n    76→    \"FileNotFoundError\",\n    77→    \"ConnectionRefusedError\",\n    78→    \"OSError\",\n    79→    \"RuntimeError\",\n    80→    \"segmentation fault\",\n    81→    \"Segmentation fault\",\n    82→    \"core dumped\",\n    83→    \"killed\",\n    84→    \"Killed\",\n    85→    \"Critical error:\",\n    86→    \"AssertionError\",\n    87→]\n    88→\n    89→# Common build artifacts that should be cleaned up\n    90→BUILD_ARTIFACT_PATTERNS = [\n    91→    \"a.out\",\n    92→    \"*.o\",\n    93→    \"*.obj\",\n    94→    \"*.exe\",\n    95→    \"*.dll\",\n    96→    \"*.so\",\n    97→    \"*.dylib\",\n    98→    \"*.pyc\",\n    99→    \"__pycache__\",\n   100→    \".DS_Store\",\n   101→    \"*.swp\",\n   102→    \"*.swo\",\n   103→    \"core\",\n   104→    \"*.core\",\n   105→]\n   106→\n   107→# Deliverable patterns - files that should NOT be cleaned\n   108→DELIVERABLE_PATTERNS = [\n   109→    \"main.py.c\",\n   110→    \"main.c.py\",\n   111→    \"move.txt\",\n   112→    \"regex.txt\",\n   113→    \"response.txt\",\n   114→    \"solution.py\",\n   115→    \"answer.txt\",\n   116→    \"output.json\",\n   117→    \"result.csv\",\n   118→]\n   119→\n   120→\n   121→def load_state() -> dict:\n   122→    if STATE_FILE.exists():\n   123→        try:\n   124→            return json.loads(STATE_FILE.read_text())\n   125→        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n   126→            pass\n   127→    return {\n   128→        \"start_time\": time.time(),\n   129→        \"turn_count\": 0,\n   130→        \"file_edits\": {},\n   131→        \"verification_attempted\": False,\n   132→        \"verification_passed\": False,\n   133→        \"consecutive_failures\": 0,\n   134→        \"last_error_sig\": \"\",\n   135→        \"stop_block_count\": 0,\n   136→    }\n   137→\n   138→\n   139→def save_state(state: dict) -> None:\n   140→    try:\n   141→        STATE_FILE.write_text(json.dumps(state))\n   142→    except OSError:\n   143→        pass\n   144→\n   145→\n   146→def emit(payload: dict) -> None:\n   147→    json.dump(payload, sys.stdout)\n   148→\n   149→\n   150→def emit_allow(reason: str = \"\") -> None:\n   151→    emit({\n   152→        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n   153→            \"hookEventName\": \"PreToolUse\",\n   154→            \"permissionDecision\": \"allow\",\n   155→            \"permissionDecisionReason\": reason,\n   156→        }\n   157→    })\n   158→\n   159→\n   160→def emit_notification(event: str, message: str) -> None:\n   161→    emit({\n   162→        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n   163→            \"hookEventName\": event,\n   164→            \"notification\": message,\n   165→        }\n   166→    })\n   167→\n   168→\n   169→def is_verification_command(cmd: str) -> bool:\n   170→    \"\"\"Check if a command is running verification tests.\"\"\"\n   171→    return any(pattern in cmd for pattern in VERIFICATION_PATTERNS)\n   172→\n   173→\n   174→def detect_hook_type(data: dict) -> str:\n   175→    if \"tool_output\" in data:\n   176→        return \"PostToolUse\"\n   177→    if \"tool_name\" in data:\n   178→        return \"PreToolUse\"\n   179→    return \"Stop\"\n   180→\n   181→\n   182→def handle_pretool(data: dict) -> None:\n   183→    state = load_state()\n   184→    state[\"turn_count\"] = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0) + 1\n   185→\n   186→    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n   187→    tool_input = data.get(\"tool_input\", {})\n   188→\n   189→    # Track file edits for loop detection\n   190→    if tool_name in (\"Edit\", \"Write\"):\n   191→        filepath = tool_input.get(\"file_path\", \"unknown\")\n   192→        state[\"file_edits\"][filepath] = state[\"file_edits\"].get(filepath, 0) + 1\n   193→\n   194→    # Track verification attempts\n   195→    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n   196→        cmd = tool_input.get(\"command\", \"\")\n   197→        if is_verification_command(cmd):\n   198→            if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n   199→                # Reset stop counter when entering verification phase\n   200→                state[\"stop_block_count\"] = 0\n   201→            state[\"verification_attempted\"] = True\n   202→\n   203→    save_state(state)\n   204→    emit_allow(\"[tbench]\")\n   205→\n   206→\n   207→def handle_posttool(data: dict) -> None:\n   208→    state = load_state()\n   209→\n   210→    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n   211→    tool_output = str(data.get(\"tool_output\", \"\"))\n   212→\n   213→    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n   214→        # Detect errors\n   215→        has_error = any(err in tool_output for err in ERROR_PATTERNS)\n   216→\n   217→        if has_error:\n   218→            sig = tool_output[:200]\n   219→            if sig == state.get(\"last_error_sig\", \"\"):\n   220→                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) + 1\n   221→            else:\n   222→                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 1\n   223→                state[\"last_error_sig\"] = sig\n   224→        else:\n   225→            state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 0\n   226→\n   227→        # Detect verification success\n   228→        if state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n   229→            if any(kw in tool_output for kw in SUCCESS_PATTERNS):\n   230→                state[\"verification_passed\"] = True\n   231→\n   232→    save_state(state)\n   233→\n   234→    # Emit warnings for loops\n   235→    notifications = []\n   236→\n   237→    for filepath, count in state.get(\"file_edits\", {}).items():\n   238→        if count >= 4:\n   239→            notifications.append(\n   240→                f\"[tbench] WARNING: {filepath} edited {count} times. \"\n   241→                \"STOP editing this file. Re-read the requirements and try \"\n   242→                \"a fundamentally different approach.\"\n   243→            )\n   244→\n   245→    if state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) >= 3:\n   246→        notifications.append(\n   247→            f\"[tbench] WARNING: {state['consecutive_failures']} consecutive failures \"\n   248→            \"with the same error. Your current approach is wrong. Switch strategy entirely.\"\n   249→        )\n   250→\n   251→    turn_count = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0)\n   252→    if turn_count >= 100 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n   253→        notifications.append(\n   254→            f\"[tbench] CRITICAL: Turn {turn_count}/200 and you haven't run verification yet. \"\n   255→            \"STOP what you're doing. Run the tests NOW. Fix only what fails.\"\n   256→        )\n   257→    elif turn_count >= 40 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n   258→        notifications.append(\n   259→            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Run verification NOW to check progress. \"\n   260→            \"Early feedback prevents wasted turns.\"\n   261→        )\n   262→    elif turn_count >= 150:\n   263→        notifications.append(\n   264→            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. You are running low on turns. \"\n   265→            \"Wrap up — ensure tests pass and stop.\"\n   266→        )\n   267→    elif turn_count >= 120 and not state.get(\"verification_passed\"):\n   268→        notifications.append(\n   269→            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Tests not yet passing. \"\n   270→            \"Simplify your approach. A minimal passing solution beats a failed ambitious one.\"\n   271→        )\n   272→\n   273→    if notifications:\n   274→        emit_notification(\"PostToolUse\", \" | \".join(notifications))\n   275→    else:\n   276→        emit({})\n   277→\n   278→\n   279→def handle_stop(data: dict) -> None:\n   280→    state = load_state()\n   281→\n   282→    if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\", False):\n   283→        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n   284→        save_state(state)\n   285→\n   286→        # After 3 blocks, let it go (avoid infinite loop)\n   287→        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 3:\n   288→            emit({})\n   289→            return\n   290→\n   291→        emit_notification(\n   292→            \"Stop\",\n   293→            \"[tbench] BLOCKED: You have NOT run the test suite. \"\n   294→            \"You MUST verify before stopping. Run: \"\n   295→            \"cd /app && bash tests/test.sh \"\n   296→            \"OR: cd /app && python3 -m pytest tests/ -v\"\n   297→        )\n   298→    elif not state.get(\"verification_passed\", False):\n   299→        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n   300→        save_state(state)\n   301→\n   302→        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 2:\n   303→            emit({})\n   304→            return\n   305→\n   306→        emit_notification(\n   307→            \"Stop\",\n   308→            \"[tbench] Tests were run but appear to have failed. \"\n   309→            \"Review the test output, fix issues, and re-run verification. \"\n   310→            \"If you believe tests actually passed, you may stop.\"\n   311→        )\n   312→    else:\n   313→        emit({})\n   314→\n   315→\n   316→def main() -> None:\n   317→    try:\n   318→        data = json.load(sys.stdin)\n   319→    except (json.JSONDecodeError, EOFError):\n   320→        data = {}\n   321→\n   322→    hook_type = detect_hook_type(data)\n   323→\n   324→    if hook_type == \"PreToolUse\":\n   325→        handle_pretool(data)\n   326→    elif hook_type == \"PostToolUse\":\n   327→        handle_posttool(data)\n   328→    elif hook_type == \"Stop\":\n   329→        handle_stop(data)\n   330→    else:\n python3\n\"\"\"\nTerminal-Bench Claude Code Hooks (unified script).\n\nHandles all three hook events via stdin payload inspection:\n- PreToolUse: tracks state, always allows\n- PostToolUse: tracks errors, verification results, loop detection\n- Stop: blocks exit if verification hasn't been attempted\n\nProtocol:\n  Input: JSON via stdin\n  Output: JSON via stdout wrapped in {\"hookSpecificOutput\": {...}}\n\nState persisted in /tmp/.tbench_state.json across invocations.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nSTATE_FILE = Path(\"/tmp/.tbench_state.json\")\n\n# Patterns that indicate the agent is running verification tests\nVERIFICATION_PATTERNS = [\n    \"tests/test.sh\",\n    \"tests/test_\",\n    \"pytest tests/\",\n    \"pytest -v\",\n    \"python -m pytest\",\n    \"python3 -m pytest\",\n    \"make test\",\n    \"cargo test\",\n    \"npm test\",\n    \"go test\",\n    \"bash test\",\n    \"./test.sh\",\n    \"ctest\",\n]\n\n# Patterns that indicate test success\nSUCCESS_PATTERNS = [\n    \"passed\",\n    \" ok\",\n    \"0 failed\",\n    \"tests passed\",\n    \"all tests\",\n    \"PASS\",\n    \"OK (\",\n    \"0 errors\",\n    \"Build succeeded\",\n    \"All checks passed\",\n]\n\n# Patterns that indicate errors in command output\nERROR_PATTERNS = [\n    \"error:\",\n    \"Error:\",\n    \"ERROR:\",\n    \"traceback\",\n    \"Traceback\",\n    \"FAILED\",\n    \"not found\",\n    \"No such file\",\n    \"permission denied\",\n    \"Permission denied\",\n    \"command not found\",\n    \"ModuleNotFoundError\",\n    \"ImportError\",\n    \"SyntaxError\",\n    \"NameError\",\n    \"TypeError\",\n    \"ValueError\",\n    \"FileNotFoundError\",\n    \"ConnectionRefusedError\",\n    \"OSError\",\n    \"RuntimeError\",\n    \"segmentation fault\",\n    \"Segmentation fault\",\n    \"core dumped\",\n    \"killed\",\n    \"Killed\",\n    \"Critical error:\",\n    \"AssertionError\",\n]\n\n# Common build artifacts that should be cleaned up\nBUILD_ARTIFACT_PATTERNS = [\n    \"a.out\",\n    \"*.o\",\n    \"*.obj\",\n    \"*.exe\",\n    \"*.dll\",\n    \"*.so\",\n    \"*.dylib\",\n    \"*.pyc\",\n    \"__pycache__\",\n    \".DS_Store\",\n    \"*.swp\",\n    \"*.swo\",\n    \"core\",\n    \"*.core\",\n]\n\n# Deliverable patterns - files that should NOT be cleaned\nDELIVERABLE_PATTERNS = [\n    \"main.py.c\",\n    \"main.c.py\",\n    \"move.txt\",\n    \"regex.txt\",\n    \"response.txt\",\n    \"solution.py\",\n    \"answer.txt\",\n    \"output.json\",\n    \"result.csv\",\n]\n\n\ndef load_state() -> dict:\n    if STATE_FILE.exists():\n        try:\n            return json.loads(STATE_FILE.read_text())\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n            pass\n    return {\n        \"start_time\": time.time(),\n        \"turn_count\": 0,\n        \"file_edits\": {},\n        \"verification_attempted\": False,\n        \"verification_passed\": False,\n        \"consecutive_failures\": 0,\n        \"last_error_sig\": \"\",\n        \"stop_block_count\": 0,\n    }\n\n\ndef save_state(state: dict) -> None:\n    try:\n        STATE_FILE.write_text(json.dumps(state))\n    except OSError:\n        pass\n\n\ndef emit(payload: dict) -> None:\n    json.dump(payload, sys.stdout)\n\n\ndef emit_allow(reason: str = \"\") -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": \"PreToolUse\",\n            \"permissionDecision\": \"allow\",\n            \"permissionDecisionReason\": reason,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef emit_notification(event: str, message: str) -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": event,\n            \"notification\": message,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef is_verification_command(cmd: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if a command is running verification tests.\"\"\"\n    return any(pattern in cmd for pattern in VERIFICATION_PATTERNS)\n\n\ndef detect_hook_type(data: dict) -> str:\n    if \"tool_output\" in data:\n        return \"PostToolUse\"\n    if \"tool_name\" in data:\n        return \"PreToolUse\"\n    return \"Stop\"\n\n\ndef handle_pretool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n    state[\"turn_count\"] = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0) + 1\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_input = data.get(\"tool_input\", {})\n\n    # Track file edits for loop detection\n    if tool_name in (\"Edit\", \"Write\"):\n        filepath = tool_input.get(\"file_path\", \"unknown\")\n        state[\"file_edits\"][filepath] = state[\"file_edits\"].get(filepath, 0) + 1\n\n    # Track verification attempts\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        cmd = tool_input.get(\"command\", \"\")\n        if is_verification_command(cmd):\n            if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n                # Reset stop counter when entering verification phase\n                state[\"stop_block_count\"] = 0\n            state[\"verification_attempted\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n    emit_allow(\"[tbench]\")\n\n\ndef handle_posttool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_output = str(data.get(\"tool_output\", \"\"))\n\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        # Detect errors\n        has_error = any(err in tool_output for err in ERROR_PATTERNS)\n\n        if has_error:\n            sig = tool_output[:200]\n            if sig == state.get(\"last_error_sig\", \"\"):\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) + 1\n            else:\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 1\n                state[\"last_error_sig\"] = sig\n        else:\n            state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 0\n\n        # Detect verification success\n        if state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n            if any(kw in tool_output for kw in SUCCESS_PATTERNS):\n                state[\"verification_passed\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n\n    # Emit warnings for loops\n    notifications = []\n\n    for filepath, count in state.get(\"file_edits\", {}).items():\n        if count >= 4:\n            notifications.append(\n                f\"[tbench] WARNING: {filepath} edited {count} times. \"\n                \"STOP editing this file. Re-read the requirements and try \"\n                \"a fundamentally different approach.\"\n            )\n\n    if state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) >= 3:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: {state['consecutive_failures']} consecutive failures \"\n            \"with the same error. Your current approach is wrong. Switch strategy entirely.\"\n        )\n\n    turn_count = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0)\n    if turn_count >= 100 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] CRITICAL: Turn {turn_count}/200 and you haven't run verification yet. \"\n            \"STOP what you're doing. Run the tests NOW. Fix only what fails.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 40 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Run verification NOW to check progress. \"\n            \"Early feedback prevents wasted turns.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 150:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. You are running low on turns. \"\n            \"Wrap up — ensure tests pass and stop.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 120 and not state.get(\"verification_passed\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Tests not yet passing. \"\n            \"Simplify your approach. A minimal passing solution beats a failed ambitious one.\"\n        )\n\n    if notifications:\n        emit_notification(\"PostToolUse\", \" | \".join(notifications))\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef handle_stop(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        # After 3 blocks, let it go (avoid infinite loop)\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 3:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] BLOCKED: You have NOT run the test suite. \"\n            \"You MUST verify before stopping. Run: \"\n            \"cd /app && bash tests/test.sh \"\n            \"OR: cd /app && python3 -m pytest tests/ -v\"\n        )\n    elif not state.get(\"verification_passed\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 2:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] Tests were run but appear to have failed. \"\n            \"Review the test output, fix issues, and re-run verification. \"\n            \"If you believe tests actually passed, you may stop.\"\n        )\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    try:\n        data = json.load(sys.stdin)\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, EOFError):\n        data = {}\n\n    hook_type = detect_hook_type(data)\n\n    if hook_type == \"PreToolUse\":\n        handle_pretool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"PostToolUse\":\n        handle_posttool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"Stop\":\n        handle_stop(data)\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n","numLines":336,"startLine":1,"totalLines":336}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01JTJzJw4TDWZB6dpHUoe2qY","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01NFAzmzWNEQpQ3YfNdz2BZT","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/ars.R","content":"##############################################################################\n# Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS)\n# Reference: Gilks, W. R., & Wild, P. (1992). Adaptive rejection sampling\n#            for Gibbs sampling. JRSS Series C, 41(2), 337-348.\n#\n# Modular implementation with input validation and log-concavity checks.\n##############################################################################\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 1: Input Validation\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Validate inputs to the ars() function.\n#' Checks types, ranges, and domain consistency.\nvalidate_inputs <- function(n, f, lb, ub, x_init) {\n  # n must be a positive integer\n  if (!is.numeric(n) || length(n) != 1 || is.na(n)) {\n    stop(\"'n' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (n < 1 || n != floor(n)) {\n    stop(\"'n' must be a positive integer.\")\n  }\n\n  # f must be a function\n  if (!is.function(f)) {\n    stop(\"'f' must be a function (density function).\")\n  }\n\n  # lb and ub must be numeric scalars (may be -Inf / Inf)\n  if (!is.numeric(lb) || length(lb) != 1 || is.na(lb)) {\n    stop(\"'lb' must be a single numeric value (may be -Inf).\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(ub) || length(ub) != 1 || is.na(ub)) {\n    stop(\"'ub' must be a single numeric value (may be Inf).\")\n  }\n  if (lb >= ub) {\n    stop(\"'lb' must be strictly less than 'ub'.\")\n  }\n\n  # x_init validation (if provided)\n  if (!is.null(x_init)) {\n    if (!is.numeric(x_init) || length(x_init) < 2) {\n      stop(\"'x_init' must be a numeric vector with at least 2 elements.\")\n    }\n    if (any(x_init <= lb) || any(x_init >= ub)) {\n      stop(\"All 'x_init' values must be strictly inside (lb, ub).\")\n    }\n  }\n\n  invisible(TRUE)\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 2: Log-density Evaluation\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Evaluate the log of the density function safely.\n#' Returns -Inf for zero or negative density values.\neval_log_density <- function(f, x) {\n  vals <- suppressWarnings(f(x))\n  if (any(is.na(vals) | is.nan(vals))) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned NA/NaN.\")\n  }\n  ifelse(vals > 0, log(vals), -Inf)\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 3: Log-Concavity Check\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Check log-concavity: for sorted abscissae x with log-density h,\n#' the slopes of the secant lines must be non-increasing.\ncheck_log_concavity <- function(x, h) {\n  if (length(x) < 3) return(invisible(TRUE))\n  slopes <- diff(h) / diff(x)\n  # Allow small numerical tolerance\n  if (any(diff(slopes) > 1e-6)) {\n    stop(\"Log-concavity violated: the supplied density does not appear to be log-concave.\")\n  }\n  invisible(TRUE)\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 4: Initialize Abscissae\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Choose starting abscissae if not supplied by user.\n#' We need at least two points in the interior of (lb, ub).\ninitialize_abscissae <- function(f, lb, ub, x_init) {\n  if (!is.null(x_init)) {\n    x_init <- sort(x_init)\n    h <- eval_log_density(f, x_init)\n    if (any(h == -Inf)) {\n      stop(\"Density is zero at one or more initial abscissae. Choose different starting points.\")\n    }\n    return(list(x = x_init, h = h))\n  }\n\n  # Automatic initialization: try to find points with positive density\n  # Strategy: try the mode region first via a grid search\n  if (is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    candidates <- seq(lb + (ub - lb) * 0.1, ub - (ub - lb) * 0.1, length.out = 20)\n  } else if (is.finite(lb)) {\n    candidates <- lb + c(0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10)\n  } else if (is.finite(ub)) {\n    candidates <- ub - c(0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10)\n  } else {\n    candidates <- c(-4, -2, -1, -0.5, 0, 0.5, 1, 2, 4)\n  }\n\n  h_cand <- eval_log_density(f, candidates)\n  good <- which(h_cand > -Inf)\n  if (length(good) < 2) {\n    stop(\"Could not find at least 2 initial points with positive density. Supply 'x_init'.\")\n  }\n\n  # Pick 2-5 well-spread points with positive density\n  sel <- good\n  if (length(sel) > 5) {\n    sel <- sel[round(seq(1, length(sel), length.out = 5))]\n  }\n\n  x_sel <- candidates[sel]\n  h_sel <- h_cand[sel]\n\n  # Ensure that for unbounded domains, the boundary slopes have correct sign\n  # If lb == -Inf, we need h'(x[1]) > 0 (increasing on the left)\n  # If ub == Inf,  we need h'(x[k]) < 0 (decreasing on the right)\n  if (!is.finite(lb) && length(x_sel) >= 2) {\n    slope_left <- (h_sel[2] - h_sel[1]) / (x_sel[2] - x_sel[1])\n    if (slope_left <= 0) {\n      # Try shifting left point further left\n      trial <- x_sel[1] - abs(x_sel[2] - x_sel[1])\n      h_trial <- eval_log_density(f, trial)\n      if (h_trial > -Inf && h_trial < h_sel[1]) {\n        x_sel <- c(trial, x_sel)\n        h_sel <- c(h_trial, h_sel)\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (!is.finite(ub) && length(x_sel) >= 2) {\n    k <- length(x_sel)\n    slope_right <- (h_sel[k] - h_sel[k - 1]) / (x_sel[k] - x_sel[k - 1])\n    if (slope_right >= 0) {\n      trial <- x_sel[k] + abs(x_sel[k] - x_sel[k - 1])\n      h_trial <- eval_log_density(f, trial)\n      if (h_trial > -Inf && h_trial < h_sel[k]) {\n        x_sel <- c(x_sel, trial)\n        h_sel <- c(h_sel, h_trial)\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  list(x = x_sel, h = h_sel)\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 5: Upper Hull (Piecewise-Linear Envelope)\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Compute the upper hull from sorted abscissae x and log-density h.\n#' Returns a list with slopes, intercepts, and intersection points z.\ncompute_upper_hull <- function(x, h, lb, ub) {\n  k <- length(x)\n  # Slopes and intercepts of tangent lines through consecutive pairs\n  slopes <- diff(h) / diff(x)\n  # Each piece j has equation: u_j(t) = h[j] + slopes[j] * (t - x[j])\n  # Or equivalently: u_j(t) = slopes[j] * t + (h[j] - slopes[j] * x[j])\n  intercepts <- h[1:(k - 1)] - slopes * x[1:(k - 1)]\n\n  # Intersection points z between consecutive pieces\n  # u_j(z) = u_{j+1}(z)\n  if (k > 2) {\n    z <- numeric(k - 2)\n    for (j in 1:(k - 2)) {\n      if (abs(slopes[j] - slopes[j + 1]) < 1e-15) {\n        z[j] <- (x[j + 1] + x[j]) / 2\n      } else {\n        z[j] <- (intercepts[j + 1] - intercepts[j]) / (slopes[j] - slopes[j + 1])\n      }\n    }\n  } else {\n    z <- numeric(0)\n  }\n\n  # The full set of breakpoints for the piecewise envelope\n  breaks <- c(lb, z, ub)\n\n  list(slopes = slopes, intercepts = intercepts, z = z, breaks = breaks)\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 6: Lower Hull (Piecewise-Linear Squeezing Function)\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Evaluate the lower hull (squeezing function) at point t.\n#' Returns -Inf outside [x[1], x[k]].\neval_lower_hull <- function(t, x, h) {\n  k <- length(x)\n  if (t < x[1] || t > x[k]) return(-Inf)\n\n  # Find which segment t falls in\n  j <- findInterval(t, x, rightmost.closed = TRUE)\n  j <- min(j, k - 1)\n  j <- max(j, 1)\n\n  # Linear interpolation\n  h[j] + (h[j + 1] - h[j]) / (x[j + 1] - x[j]) * (t - x[j])\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 7: Evaluate Upper Hull at a Point\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Evaluate the upper hull at point t.\neval_upper_hull <- function(t, hull, x, h) {\n  # Find which piece t belongs to\n  j <- findInterval(t, hull$breaks, rightmost.closed = TRUE)\n  j <- max(1, min(j, length(hull$slopes)))\n  hull$slopes[j] * t + hull$intercepts[j]\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 8: Sample from the Upper Hull (Piecewise-Exponential)\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Sample one point from the piecewise-exponential distribution defined\n#' by the upper hull. Also returns the log of the proposal density\n#' (unnormalized) for the acceptance test.\nsample_upper_hull <- function(hull, x, h, lb, ub) {\n  k <- length(hull$slopes)\n  breaks <- hull$breaks\n\n  # Compute (unnormalized) integral of exp(u(t)) on each piece\n  log_integrals <- numeric(k)\n  for (j in 1:k) {\n    a <- breaks[j]\n    b <- breaks[j + 1]\n    sj <- hull$slopes[j]\n    cj <- hull$intercepts[j]\n\n    if (abs(sj) < 1e-12) {\n      # Nearly flat: integral = exp(cj) * (b - a)\n      if (is.finite(a) && is.finite(b)) {\n        log_integrals[j] <- cj + log(b - a)\n      } else {\n        log_integrals[j] <- -Inf  # Improper with zero slope — shouldn't happen if log-concave\n      }\n    } else {\n      # Integral of exp(sj * t + cj) from a to b = exp(cj)/sj * [exp(sj*b) - exp(sj*a)]\n      if (!is.finite(a)) {\n        # a = -Inf: need sj > 0 for convergence\n        if (sj > 0) {\n          log_integrals[j] <- cj + sj * b - log(sj)\n        } else {\n          log_integrals[j] <- -Inf\n        }\n      } else if (!is.finite(b)) {\n        # b = Inf: need sj < 0 for convergence\n        if (sj < 0) {\n          log_integrals[j] <- cj + sj * a - log(-sj)\n        } else {\n          log_integrals[j] <- -Inf\n        }\n      } else {\n        # Both finite\n        log_val_b <- sj * b\n        log_val_a <- sj * a\n        log_diff <- log_val_b + log(1 - exp(log_val_a - log_val_b))\n        if (sj > 0) {\n          log_integrals[j] <- cj + log_diff - log(sj)\n        } else {\n          log_integrals[j] <- cj + log_val_a + log(1 - exp(log_val_b - log_val_a)) - log(-sj)\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  # Normalize to get piece probabilities\n  max_li <- max(log_integrals[is.finite(log_integrals)])\n  weights <- exp(log_integrals - max_li)\n  weights[!is.finite(weights)] <- 0\n  probs <- weights / sum(weights)\n\n  # Select a piece\n  j <- sample.int(k, size = 1, prob = probs)\n\n  a <- breaks[j]\n  b <- breaks[j + 1]\n  sj <- hull$slopes[j]\n  cj <- hull$intercepts[j]\n\n  # Sample from truncated exponential on [a, b] with rate sj\n  u <- runif(1)\n  if (abs(sj) < 1e-12) {\n    # Uniform on [a, b]\n    t_star <- a + u * (b - a)\n  } else {\n    if (!is.finite(a)) {\n      # a = -Inf, sj > 0: CDF = exp(sj*(t - b)), sample: t = b + log(u)/sj\n      t_star <- b + log(u) / sj\n    } else if (!is.finite(b)) {\n      # b = Inf, sj < 0: CDF = 1 - exp(sj*(t - a)), sample: t = a + log(1-u)/sj ...\n      # Actually: t = a - log(1 - u) / (-sj) = a + log(1-u)/sj\n      t_star <- a + log(1 - u) / sj\n    } else {\n      # Inverse CDF for truncated exponential\n      exp_a <- exp(sj * a)\n      exp_b <- exp(sj * b)\n      t_star <- log(exp_a + u * (exp_b - exp_a)) / sj\n    }\n  }\n\n  # Log-density of the upper hull at t_star\n  log_u_star <- sj * t_star + cj\n\n  list(x_star = t_star, log_u = log_u_star)\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 9: Update Step (Add New Abscissa)\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Insert a new abscissa into the sorted set and recompute hulls.\nupdate_abscissae <- function(x_star, h_star, x, h) {\n  pos <- findInterval(x_star, x) + 1\n  x_new <- append(x, x_star, after = pos - 1)\n  h_new <- append(h, h_star, after = pos - 1)\n  list(x = x_new, h = h_new)\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 10: Main ARS Function\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Adaptive Rejection Sampling\n#'\n#' @param n       Number of samples to draw (positive integer).\n#' @param f       Density function (possibly unnormalized). Must be vectorized.\n#' @param lb      Lower bound of the support (may be -Inf).\n#' @param ub      Upper bound of the support (may be Inf).\n#' @param x_init  Optional numeric vector of initial abscissae (at least 2).\n#'                Must lie strictly inside (lb, ub).\n#' @return Numeric vector of n samples from f.\nars <- function(n, f, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf, x_init = NULL) {\n\n  # --- Step 1: Validate inputs ---\n  validate_inputs(n, f, lb, ub, x_init)\n\n  # --- Step 2: Initialize abscissae ---\n  init <- initialize_abscissae(f, lb, ub, x_init)\n  x_abs <- init$x\n  h_abs <- init$h\n\n  # --- Step 3: Check initial log-concavity ---\n  check_log_concavity(x_abs, h_abs)\n\n  # --- Step 4: Sampling loop ---\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  i <- 1\n\n  while (i <= n) {\n    # Compute upper hull\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(x_abs, h_abs, lb, ub)\n\n    # Sample from upper hull\n    proposal <- sample_upper_hull(hull, x_abs, h_abs, lb, ub)\n    x_star <- proposal$x_star\n    log_u_star <- proposal$log_u\n\n    # Evaluate lower hull (squeezing test)\n    l_star <- eval_lower_hull(x_star, x_abs, h_abs)\n\n    w <- runif(1)\n\n    if (log(w) <= l_star - log_u_star) {\n      # Accepted by squeezing test — no density evaluation needed\n      samples[i] <- x_star\n      i <- i + 1\n    } else {\n      # Need to evaluate the true log-density\n      h_star <- eval_log_density(f, x_star)\n\n      if (log(w) <= h_star - log_u_star) {\n        # Accepted by rejection test\n        samples[i] <- x_star\n        i <- i + 1\n      }\n\n      # Update abscissae with the new point\n      updated <- update_abscissae(x_star, h_star, x_abs, h_abs)\n      x_abs <- updated$x\n      h_abs <- updated$h\n\n      # Check log-concavity with new point\n      check_log_concavity(x_abs, h_abs)\n    }\n  }\n\n  samples\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 11: Test Function\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Run formal tests comparing ARS output against known distributions.\n#' Uses Kolmogorov-Smirnov tests and moment checks.\ntest <- function() {\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n  cat(\"  Adaptive Rejection Sampler (ARS) — Test Suite\\n\")\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\\n\")\n\n  n_samples <- 5000\n  ks_alpha <- 0.001  # Very conservative to avoid false failures\n\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  # Helper: run a single distribution test\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  run_distribution_test <- function(test_name, f, lb, ub, ref_cdf,\n                                     true_mean, true_sd, x_init = NULL) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"Testing: %s\\n\", test_name))\n\n    result <- tryCatch({\n      samples <- ars(n = n_samples, f = f, lb = lb, ub = ub, x_init = x_init)\n\n      # KS test against reference CDF\n      ks <- ks.test(samples, ref_cdf)\n\n      # Moment checks\n      samp_mean <- mean(samples)\n      samp_sd <- sd(samples)\n\n      cat(sprintf(\"  Sample mean = %.4f (true = %.4f)\\n\", samp_mean, true_mean))\n      cat(sprintf(\"  Sample SD   = %.4f (true = %.4f)\\n\", samp_sd, true_sd))\n      cat(sprintf(\"  KS p-value  = %.4f\\n\", ks$p.value))\n\n      # Pass conditions: KS test not rejected AND moments are reasonable\n      mean_ok <- abs(samp_mean - true_mean) < 3 * true_sd / sqrt(n_samples) + 0.1\n      sd_ok <- abs(samp_sd - true_sd) / true_sd < 0.2\n      ks_ok <- ks$p.value > ks_alpha\n\n      if (ks_ok && mean_ok && sd_ok) {\n        cat(sprintf(\"%s: PASS\\n\\n\", test_name))\n        return(list(passed = TRUE, samples = samples))\n      } else {\n        reasons <- c()\n        if (!ks_ok) reasons <- c(reasons, \"KS test rejected\")\n        if (!mean_ok) reasons <- c(reasons, \"mean off\")\n        if (!sd_ok) reasons <- c(reasons, \"SD off\")\n        cat(sprintf(\"%s: FAIL (%s)\\n\\n\", test_name, paste(reasons, collapse = \", \")))\n        return(list(passed = FALSE, samples = samples))\n      }\n    }, error = function(e) {\n      cat(sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", e$message))\n      cat(sprintf(\"%s: FAIL (error)\\n\\n\", test_name))\n      return(list(passed = FALSE, samples = NULL))\n    })\n\n    result\n  }\n\n  passed <- 0\n  total <- 0\n\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  # Test 1: Standard Normal N(0,1)\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  total <- total + 1\n  res <- run_distribution_test(\n    test_name = \"Standard_Normal\",\n    f = dnorm,\n    lb = -Inf, ub = Inf,\n    ref_cdf = pnorm,\n    true_mean = 0, true_sd = 1\n  )\n  if (res$passed) passed <- passed + 1\n  # Save samples\n  if (!is.null(res$samples)) {\n    writeLines(as.character(res$samples), \"/app/normal_samples.txt\")\n  }\n\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  # Test 2: Normal(3, 2) — shifted/scaled\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  total <- total + 1\n  res <- run_distribution_test(\n    test_name = \"Normal_mu3_sd2\",\n    f = function(x) dnorm(x, mean = 3, sd = 2),\n    lb = -Inf, ub = Inf,\n    ref_cdf = function(x) pnorm(x, mean = 3, sd = 2),\n    true_mean = 3, true_sd = 2\n  )\n  if (res$passed) passed <- passed + 1\n\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  # Test 3: Exponential(1) on (0, Inf)\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  total <- total + 1\n  res <- run_distribution_test(\n    test_name = \"Exponential_rate1\",\n    f = dexp,\n    lb = 0, ub = Inf,\n    ref_cdf = pexp,\n    true_mean = 1, true_sd = 1,\n    x_init = c(0.5, 1, 3)\n  )\n  if (res$passed) passed <- passed + 1\n  # Save exponential samples\n  if (!is.null(res$samples)) {\n    writeLines(as.character(res$samples), \"/app/exponential_samples.txt\")\n  }\n\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  # Test 4: Gamma(5, 1) — shape > 1, log-concave\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  total <- total + 1\n  res <- run_distribution_test(\n    test_name = \"Gamma_shape5_rate1\",\n    f = function(x) dgamma(x, shape = 5, rate = 1),\n    lb = 0, ub = Inf,\n    ref_cdf = function(x) pgamma(x, shape = 5, rate = 1),\n    true_mean = 5, true_sd = sqrt(5),\n    x_init = c(1, 3, 5, 8)\n  )\n  if (res$passed) passed <- passed + 1\n\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  # Test 5: Beta(2, 5) on (0, 1)\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  total <- total + 1\n  res <- run_distribution_test(\n    test_name = \"Beta_2_5\",\n    f = function(x) dbeta(x, 2, 5),\n    lb = 0, ub = 1,\n    ref_cdf = function(x) pbeta(x, 2, 5),\n    true_mean = 2 / 7, true_sd = sqrt(10 / (49 * 8)),\n    x_init = c(0.1, 0.3, 0.6)\n  )\n  if (res$passed) passed <- passed + 1\n\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  # Test 6: Truncated Normal on (-2, 2)\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  total <- total + 1\n  # Truncated N(0,1) on (-2,2): mean=0, sd ~ 0.72\n  Z <- pnorm(2) - pnorm(-2)\n  trunc_sd <- sqrt(1 - 2 * 2 * dnorm(2) / Z)\n  res <- run_distribution_test(\n    test_name = \"Truncated_Normal_-2_2\",\n    f = dnorm,\n    lb = -2, ub = 2,\n    ref_cdf = function(x) {\n      (pnorm(pmin(pmax(x, -2), 2)) - pnorm(-2)) / Z\n    },\n    true_mean = 0, true_sd = trunc_sd,\n    x_init = c(-1, 0, 1)\n  )\n  if (res$passed) passed <- passed + 1\n\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  # Test 7: Logistic distribution\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  total <- total + 1\n  res <- run_distribution_test(\n    test_name = \"Logistic_0_1\",\n    f = dlogis,\n    lb = -Inf, ub = Inf,\n    ref_cdf = plogis,\n    true_mean = 0, true_sd = pi / sqrt(3)\n  )\n  if (res$passed) passed <- passed + 1\n\n  # ==================================================================\n  # Input Validation Tests\n  # ==================================================================\n  cat(\"--- Input Validation Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  # Test: negative n\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Negative_n_rejected\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    ars(n = -5, f = dnorm)\n    cat(\"Negative_n_rejected: FAIL (no error raised)\\n\\n\")\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Caught: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Negative_n_rejected: PASS\\n\\n\")\n    passed <<- passed + 1\n  })\n\n  # Test: non-function f\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Non_function_f_rejected\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    ars(n = 100, f = \"not_a_function\")\n    cat(\"Non_function_f_rejected: FAIL (no error raised)\\n\\n\")\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Caught: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Non_function_f_rejected: PASS\\n\\n\")\n    passed <<- passed + 1\n  })\n\n  # Test: lb >= ub\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Invalid_domain_rejected\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    ars(n = 100, f = dnorm, lb = 5, ub = 2)\n    cat(\"Invalid_domain_rejected: FAIL (no error raised)\\n\\n\")\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Caught: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Invalid_domain_rejected: PASS\\n\\n\")\n    passed <<- passed + 1\n  })\n\n  # Test: non-log-concave density (Cauchy)\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Non_log_concave_rejected\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    ars(n = 100, f = dcauchy, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf, x_init = c(-5, -1, 0, 1, 5))\n    cat(\"Non_log_concave_rejected: FAIL (no error raised)\\n\\n\")\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Caught: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Non_log_concave_rejected: PASS\\n\\n\")\n    passed <<- passed + 1\n  })\n\n  # Test: n = 0\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Zero_n_rejected\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    ars(n = 0, f = dnorm)\n    cat(\"Zero_n_rejected: FAIL (no error raised)\\n\\n\")\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Caught: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Zero_n_rejected: PASS\\n\\n\")\n    passed <<- passed + 1\n  })\n\n  # Test: x_init outside domain\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Init_outside_domain_rejected\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    ars(n = 100, f = dnorm, lb = 0, ub = 10, x_init = c(-1, 5))\n    cat(\"Init_outside_domain_rejected: FAIL (no error raised)\\n\\n\")\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Caught: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Init_outside_domain_rejected: PASS\\n\\n\")\n    passed <<- passed + 1\n  })\n\n  # ==================================================================\n  # Module Tests\n  # ==================================================================\n  cat(\"--- Module Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  # Test: upper/lower hull evaluation\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Hull_evaluation\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    x_test <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_test)\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(x_test, h_test, -Inf, Inf)\n\n    # Upper hull must be >= log-density at all abscissae\n    for (xi in seq(-3, 3, by = 0.5)) {\n      u_val <- eval_upper_hull(xi, hull, x_test, h_test)\n      h_val <- eval_log_density(dnorm, xi)\n      if (u_val < h_val - 1e-6) {\n        stop(sprintf(\"Upper hull below log-density at x=%.2f: u=%.4f < h=%.4f\", xi, u_val, h_val))\n      }\n    }\n\n    # Lower hull must be <= log-density at interior points\n    for (xi in seq(-1.5, 1.5, by = 0.5)) {\n      l_val <- eval_lower_hull(xi, x_test, h_test)\n      h_val <- eval_log_density(dnorm, xi)\n      if (l_val > h_val + 1e-6) {\n        stop(sprintf(\"Lower hull above log-density at x=%.2f: l=%.4f > h=%.4f\", xi, l_val, h_val))\n      }\n    }\n\n    cat(\"  Upper hull >= log-density: OK\\n\")\n    cat(\"  Lower hull <= log-density: OK\\n\")\n    cat(\"Hull_evaluation: PASS\\n\\n\")\n    passed <<- passed + 1\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Hull_evaluation: FAIL\\n\\n\")\n  })\n\n  # Test: log-concavity detection\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Log_concavity_detection\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    # Non-log-concave: bimodal mixture\n    x_bimodal <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n    h_bimodal <- log(0.5 * dnorm(x_bimodal, -2) + 0.5 * dnorm(x_bimodal, 2))\n\n    err_caught <- FALSE\n    tryCatch({\n      check_log_concavity(x_bimodal, h_bimodal)\n    }, error = function(e) {\n      err_caught <<- TRUE\n    })\n\n    if (err_caught) {\n      cat(\"  Bimodal density correctly rejected\\n\")\n      cat(\"Log_concavity_detection: PASS\\n\\n\")\n      passed <<- passed + 1\n    } else {\n      cat(\"  Bimodal density was NOT rejected\\n\")\n      cat(\"Log_concavity_detection: FAIL\\n\\n\")\n    }\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Log_concavity_detection: FAIL\\n\\n\")\n  })\n\n  # Test: update_abscissae maintains sorted order\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Update_abscissae_sorted\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    x0 <- c(1, 3, 5, 7)\n    h0 <- c(-1, -0.5, -0.5, -1)\n    upd <- update_abscissae(4, -0.6, x0, h0)\n    if (all(diff(upd$x) > 0) && length(upd$x) == 5 && upd$x[3] == 4) {\n      cat(\"  Sorted order maintained after insertion\\n\")\n      cat(\"Update_abscissae_sorted: PASS\\n\\n\")\n      passed <<- passed + 1\n    } else {\n      cat(\"Update_abscissae_sorted: FAIL\\n\\n\")\n    }\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Update_abscissae_sorted: FAIL\\n\\n\")\n  })\n\n  # ==================================================================\n  # Summary\n  # ==================================================================\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n  cat(sprintf(\"  Results: %d / %d tests passed\\n\", passed, total))\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n\n  invisible(passed == total)\n}\n\n\n# Run tests if this script is executed directly\nif (!interactive() && identical(sys.frame(1)$ofile, NULL)) {\n  # Only auto-run if sourced with --args test or run directly\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":390,"cache_read_input_tokens":27834,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":390,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8b99dc74-7309-4fed-a44e-e826f7a44ba3","uuid":"79df7532-8cf7-4f9a-87b5-d552840c11c6"}

R., & Wild, P. (1992). Adaptive rejection sampling\n#            for Gibbs sampling. JRSS Series C, 41(2), 337-348.\n#\n# Modular implementation with input validation and log-concavity checks.\n##############################################################################\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 1: Input Validation\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Validate inputs to the ars() function.\n#' Checks types, ranges, and domain consistency.\nvalidate_inputs <- function(n, f, lb, ub, x_init) {\n  # n must be a positive integer\n  if (!is.numeric(n) || length(n) != 1 || is.na(n)) {\n    stop(\"'n' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (n < 1 || n != floor(n)) {\n    stop(\"'n' must be a positive integer.\")\n  }\n\n  # f must be a function\n  if (!is.function(f)) {\n    stop(\"'f' must be a function (density function).\")\n  }\n\n  # lb and ub must be numeric scalars (may be -Inf / Inf)\n  if (!is.numeric(lb) || length(lb) != 1 || is.na(lb)) {\n    stop(\"'lb' must be a single numeric value (may be -Inf).\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(ub) || length(ub) != 1 || is.na(ub)) {\n    stop(\"'ub' must be a single numeric value (may be Inf).\")\n  }\n  if (lb >= ub) {\n    stop(\"'lb' must be strictly less than 'ub'.\")\n  }\n\n  # x_init validation (if provided)\n  if (!is.null(x_init)) {\n    if (!is.numeric(x_init) || length(x_init) < 2) {\n      stop(\"'x_init' must be a numeric vector with at least 2 elements.\")\n    }\n    if (any(x_init <= lb) || any(x_init >= ub)) {\n      stop(\"All 'x_init' values must be strictly inside (lb, ub).\")\n    }\n  }\n\n  invisible(TRUE)\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 2: Log-density Evaluation\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Evaluate the log of the density function safely.\n#' Returns -Inf for zero or negative density values.\neval_log_density <- function(f, x) {\n  vals <- suppressWarnings(f(x))\n  if (any(is.na(vals) | is.nan(vals))) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned NA/NaN.\")\n  }\n  ifelse(vals > 0, log(vals), -Inf)\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 3: Log-Concavity Check\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Check log-concavity: for sorted abscissae x with log-density h,\n#' the slopes of the secant lines must be non-increasing.\ncheck_log_concavity <- function(x, h) {\n  if (length(x) < 3) return(invisible(TRUE))\n  slopes <- diff(h) / diff(x)\n  # Allow small numerical tolerance\n  if (any(diff(slopes) > 1e-6)) {\n    stop(\"Log-concavity violated: the supplied density does not appear to be log-concave.\")\n  }\n  invisible(TRUE)\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 4: Initialize Abscissae\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Choose starting abscissae if not supplied by user.\n#' We need at least two points in the interior of (lb, ub).\ninitialize_abscissae <- function(f, lb, ub, x_init) {\n  if (!is.null(x_init)) {\n    x_init <- sort(x_init)\n    h <- eval_log_density(f, x_init)\n    if (any(h == -Inf)) {\n      stop(\"Density is zero at one or more initial abscissae. Choose different starting points.\")\n    }\n    return(list(x = x_init, h = h))\n  }\n\n  # Automatic initialization: try to find points with positive density\n  # Strategy: try the mode region first via a grid search\n  if (is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    candidates <- seq(lb + (ub - lb) * 0.1, ub - (ub - lb) * 0.1, length.out = 20)\n  } else if (is.finite(lb)) {\n    candidates <- lb + c(0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10)\n  } else if (is.finite(ub)) {\n    candidates <- ub - c(0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10)\n  } else {\n    candidates <- c(-4, -2, -1, -0.5, 0, 0.5, 1, 2, 4)\n  }\n\n  h_cand <- eval_log_density(f, candidates)\n  good <- which(h_cand > -Inf)\n  if (length(good) < 2) {\n    stop(\"Could not find at least 2 initial points with positive density. Supply 'x_init'.\")\n  }\n\n  # Pick 2-5 well-spread points with positive density\n  sel <- good\n  if (length(sel) > 5) {\n    sel <- sel[round(seq(1, length(sel), length.out = 5))]\n  }\n\n  x_sel <- candidates[sel]\n  h_sel <- h_cand[sel]\n\n  # Ensure that for unbounded domains, the boundary slopes have correct sign\n  # If lb == -Inf, we need h'(x[1]) > 0 (increasing on the left)\n  # If ub == Inf,  we need h'(x[k]) < 0 (decreasing on the right)\n  if (!is.finite(lb) && length(x_sel) >= 2) {\n    slope_left <- (h_sel[2] - h_sel[1]) / (x_sel[2] - x_sel[1])\n    if (slope_left <= 0) {\n      # Try shifting left point further left\n      trial <- x_sel[1] - abs(x_sel[2] - x_sel[1])\n      h_trial <- eval_log_density(f, trial)\n      if (h_trial > -Inf && h_trial < h_sel[1]) {\n        x_sel <- c(trial, x_sel)\n        h_sel <- c(h_trial, h_sel)\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (!is.finite(ub) && length(x_sel) >= 2) {\n    k <- length(x_sel)\n    slope_right <- (h_sel[k] - h_sel[k - 1]) / (x_sel[k] - x_sel[k - 1])\n    if (slope_right >= 0) {\n      trial <- x_sel[k] + abs(x_sel[k] - x_sel[k - 1])\n      h_trial <- eval_log_density(f, trial)\n      if (h_trial > -Inf && h_trial < h_sel[k]) {\n        x_sel <- c(x_sel, trial)\n        h_sel <- c(h_sel, h_trial)\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  list(x = x_sel, h = h_sel)\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 5: Upper Hull (Piecewise-Linear Envelope)\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Compute the upper hull from sorted abscissae x and log-density h.\n#' Returns a list with slopes, intercepts, and intersection points z.\ncompute_upper_hull <- function(x, h, lb, ub) {\n  k <- length(x)\n  # Slopes and intercepts of tangent lines through consecutive pairs\n  slopes <- diff(h) / diff(x)\n  # Each piece j has equation: u_j(t) = h[j] + slopes[j] * (t - x[j])\n  # Or equivalently: u_j(t) = slopes[j] * t + (h[j] - slopes[j] * x[j])\n  intercepts <- h[1:(k - 1)] - slopes * x[1:(k - 1)]\n\n  # Intersection points z between consecutive pieces\n  # u_j(z) = u_{j+1}(z)\n  if (k > 2) {\n    z <- numeric(k - 2)\n    for (j in 1:(k - 2)) {\n      if (abs(slopes[j] - slopes[j + 1]) < 1e-15) {\n        z[j] <- (x[j + 1] + x[j]) / 2\n      } else {\n        z[j] <- (intercepts[j + 1] - intercepts[j]) / (slopes[j] - slopes[j + 1])\n      }\n    }\n  } else {\n    z <- numeric(0)\n  }\n\n  # The full set of breakpoints for the piecewise envelope\n  breaks <- c(lb, z, ub)\n\n  list(slopes = slopes, intercepts = intercepts, z = z, breaks = breaks)\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 6: Lower Hull (Piecewise-Linear Squeezing Function)\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Evaluate the lower hull (squeezing function) at point t.\n#' Returns -Inf outside [x[1], x[k]].\neval_lower_hull <- function(t, x, h) {\n  k <- length(x)\n  if (t < x[1] || t > x[k]) return(-Inf)\n\n  # Find which segment t falls in\n  j <- findInterval(t, x, rightmost.closed = TRUE)\n  j <- min(j, k - 1)\n  j <- max(j, 1)\n\n  # Linear interpolation\n  h[j] + (h[j + 1] - h[j]) / (x[j + 1] - x[j]) * (t - x[j])\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 7: Evaluate Upper Hull at a Point\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Evaluate the upper hull at point t.\neval_upper_hull <- function(t, hull, x, h) {\n  # Find which piece t belongs to\n  j <- findInterval(t, hull$breaks, rightmost.closed = TRUE)\n  j <- max(1, min(j, length(hull$slopes)))\n  hull$slopes[j] * t + hull$intercepts[j]\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 8: Sample from the Upper Hull (Piecewise-Exponential)\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Sample one point from the piecewise-exponential distribution defined\n#' by the upper hull. Also returns the log of the proposal density\n#' (unnormalized) for the acceptance test.\nsample_upper_hull <- function(hull, x, h, lb, ub) {\n  k <- length(hull$slopes)\n  breaks <- hull$breaks\n\n  # Compute (unnormalized) integral of exp(u(t)) on each piece\n  log_integrals <- numeric(k)\n  for (j in 1:k) {\n    a <- breaks[j]\n    b <- breaks[j + 1]\n    sj <- hull$slopes[j]\n    cj <- hull$intercepts[j]\n\n    if (abs(sj) < 1e-12) {\n      # Nearly flat: integral = exp(cj) * (b - a)\n      if (is.finite(a) && is.finite(b)) {\n        log_integrals[j] <- cj + log(b - a)\n      } else {\n        log_integrals[j] <- -Inf  # Improper with zero slope — shouldn't happen if log-concave\n      }\n    } else {\n      # Integral of exp(sj * t + cj) from a to b = exp(cj)/sj * [exp(sj*b) - exp(sj*a)]\n      if (!is.finite(a)) {\n        # a = -Inf: need sj > 0 for convergence\n        if (sj > 0) {\n          log_integrals[j] <- cj + sj * b - log(sj)\n        } else {\n          log_integrals[j] <- -Inf\n        }\n      } else if (!is.finite(b)) {\n        # b = Inf: need sj < 0 for convergence\n        if (sj < 0) {\n          log_integrals[j] <- cj + sj * a - log(-sj)\n        } else {\n          log_integrals[j] <- -Inf\n        }\n      } else {\n        # Both finite\n        log_val_b <- sj * b\n        log_val_a <- sj * a\n        log_diff <- log_val_b + log(1 - exp(log_val_a - log_val_b))\n        if (sj > 0) {\n          log_integrals[j] <- cj + log_diff - log(sj)\n        } else {\n          log_integrals[j] <- cj + log_val_a + log(1 - exp(log_val_b - log_val_a)) - log(-sj)\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  # Normalize to get piece probabilities\n  max_li <- max(log_integrals[is.finite(log_integrals)])\n  weights <- exp(log_integrals - max_li)\n  weights[!is.finite(weights)] <- 0\n  probs <- weights / sum(weights)\n\n  # Select a piece\n  j <- sample.int(k, size = 1, prob = probs)\n\n  a <- breaks[j]\n  b <- breaks[j + 1]\n  sj <- hull$slopes[j]\n  cj <- hull$intercepts[j]\n\n  # Sample from truncated exponential on [a, b] with rate sj\n  u <- runif(1)\n  if (abs(sj) < 1e-12) {\n    # Uniform on [a, b]\n    t_star <- a + u * (b - a)\n  } else {\n    if (!is.finite(a)) {\n      # a = -Inf, sj > 0: CDF = exp(sj*(t - b)), sample: t = b + log(u)/sj\n      t_star <- b + log(u) / sj\n    } else if (!is.finite(b)) {\n      # b = Inf, sj < 0: CDF = 1 - exp(sj*(t - a)), sample: t = a + log(1-u)/sj ...\n      # Actually: t = a - log(1 - u) / (-sj) = a + log(1-u)/sj\n      t_star <- a + log(1 - u) / sj\n    } else {\n      # Inverse CDF for truncated exponential\n      exp_a <- exp(sj * a)\n      exp_b <- exp(sj * b)\n      t_star <- log(exp_a + u * (exp_b - exp_a)) / sj\n    }\n  }\n\n  # Log-density of the upper hull at t_star\n  log_u_star <- sj * t_star + cj\n\n  list(x_star = t_star, log_u = log_u_star)\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 9: Update Step (Add New Abscissa)\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Insert a new abscissa into the sorted set and recompute hulls.\nupdate_abscissae <- function(x_star, h_star, x, h) {\n  pos <- findInterval(x_star, x) + 1\n  x_new <- append(x, x_star, after = pos - 1)\n  h_new <- append(h, h_star, after = pos - 1)\n  list(x = x_new, h = h_new)\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 10: Main ARS Function\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Adaptive Rejection Sampling\n#'\n#' @param n       Number of samples to draw (positive integer).\n#' @param f       Density function (possibly unnormalized). Must be vectorized.\n#' @param lb      Lower bound of the support (may be -Inf).\n#' @param ub      Upper bound of the support (may be Inf).\n#' @param x_init  Optional numeric vector of initial abscissae (at least 2).\n#'                Must lie strictly inside (lb, ub).\n#' @return Numeric vector of n samples from f.\nars <- function(n, f, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf, x_init = NULL) {\n\n  # --- Step 1: Validate inputs ---\n  validate_inputs(n, f, lb, ub, x_init)\n\n  # --- Step 2: Initialize abscissae ---\n  init <- initialize_abscissae(f, lb, ub, x_init)\n  x_abs <- init$x\n  h_abs <- init$h\n\n  # --- Step 3: Check initial log-concavity ---\n  check_log_concavity(x_abs, h_abs)\n\n  # --- Step 4: Sampling loop ---\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  i <- 1\n\n  while (i <= n) {\n    # Compute upper hull\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(x_abs, h_abs, lb, ub)\n\n    # Sample from upper hull\n    proposal <- sample_upper_hull(hull, x_abs, h_abs, lb, ub)\n    x_star <- proposal$x_star\n    log_u_star <- proposal$log_u\n\n    # Evaluate lower hull (squeezing test)\n    l_star <- eval_lower_hull(x_star, x_abs, h_abs)\n\n    w <- runif(1)\n\n    if (log(w) <= l_star - log_u_star) {\n      # Accepted by squeezing test — no density evaluation needed\n      samples[i] <- x_star\n      i <- i + 1\n    } else {\n      # Need to evaluate the true log-density\n      h_star <- eval_log_density(f, x_star)\n\n      if (log(w) <= h_star - log_u_star) {\n        # Accepted by rejection test\n        samples[i] <- x_star\n        i <- i + 1\n      }\n\n      # Update abscissae with the new point\n      updated <- update_abscissae(x_star, h_star, x_abs, h_abs)\n      x_abs <- updated$x\n      h_abs <- updated$h\n\n      # Check log-concavity with new point\n      check_log_concavity(x_abs, h_abs)\n    }\n  }\n\n  samples\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module 11: Test Function\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#' Run formal tests comparing ARS output against known distributions.\n#' Uses Kolmogorov-Smirnov tests and moment checks.\ntest <- function() {\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n  cat(\"  Adaptive Rejection Sampler (ARS) — Test Suite\\n\")\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\\n\")\n\n  n_samples <- 5000\n  ks_alpha <- 0.001  # Very conservative to avoid false failures\n\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  # Helper: run a single distribution test\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  run_distribution_test <- function(test_name, f, lb, ub, ref_cdf,\n                                     true_mean, true_sd, x_init = NULL) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"Testing: %s\\n\", test_name))\n\n    result <- tryCatch({\n      samples <- ars(n = n_samples, f = f, lb = lb, ub = ub, x_init = x_init)\n\n      # KS test against reference CDF\n      ks <- ks.test(samples, ref_cdf)\n\n      # Moment checks\n      samp_mean <- mean(samples)\n      samp_sd <- sd(samples)\n\n      cat(sprintf(\"  Sample mean = %.4f (true = %.4f)\\n\", samp_mean, true_mean))\n      cat(sprintf(\"  Sample SD   = %.4f (true = %.4f)\\n\", samp_sd, true_sd))\n      cat(sprintf(\"  KS p-value  = %.4f\\n\", ks$p.value))\n\n      # Pass conditions: KS test not rejected AND moments are reasonable\n      mean_ok <- abs(samp_mean - true_mean) < 3 * true_sd / sqrt(n_samples) + 0.1\n      sd_ok <- abs(samp_sd - true_sd) / true_sd < 0.2\n      ks_ok <- ks$p.value > ks_alpha\n\n      if (ks_ok && mean_ok && sd_ok) {\n        cat(sprintf(\"%s: PASS\\n\\n\", test_name))\n        return(list(passed = TRUE, samples = samples))\n      } else {\n        reasons <- c()\n        if (!ks_ok) reasons <- c(reasons, \"KS test rejected\")\n        if (!mean_ok) reasons <- c(reasons, \"mean off\")\n        if (!sd_ok) reasons <- c(reasons, \"SD off\")\n        cat(sprintf(\"%s: FAIL (%s)\\n\\n\", test_name, paste(reasons, collapse = \", \")))\n        return(list(passed = FALSE, samples = samples))\n      }\n    }, error = function(e) {\n      cat(sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", e$message))\n      cat(sprintf(\"%s: FAIL (error)\\n\\n\", test_name))\n      return(list(passed = FALSE, samples = NULL))\n    })\n\n    result\n  }\n\n  passed <- 0\n  total <- 0\n\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  # Test 1: Standard Normal N(0,1)\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  total <- total + 1\n  res <- run_distribution_test(\n    test_name = \"Standard_Normal\",\n    f = dnorm,\n    lb = -Inf, ub = Inf,\n    ref_cdf = pnorm,\n    true_mean = 0, true_sd = 1\n  )\n  if (res$passed) passed <- passed + 1\n  # Save samples\n  if (!is.null(res$samples)) {\n    writeLines(as.character(res$samples), \"/app/normal_samples.txt\")\n  }\n\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  # Test 2: Normal(3, 2) — shifted/scaled\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  total <- total + 1\n  res <- run_distribution_test(\n    test_name = \"Normal_mu3_sd2\",\n    f = function(x) dnorm(x, mean = 3, sd = 2),\n    lb = -Inf, ub = Inf,\n    ref_cdf = function(x) pnorm(x, mean = 3, sd = 2),\n    true_mean = 3, true_sd = 2\n  )\n  if (res$passed) passed <- passed + 1\n\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  # Test 3: Exponential(1) on (0, Inf)\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  total <- total + 1\n  res <- run_distribution_test(\n    test_name = \"Exponential_rate1\",\n    f = dexp,\n    lb = 0, ub = Inf,\n    ref_cdf = pexp,\n    true_mean = 1, true_sd = 1,\n    x_init = c(0.5, 1, 3)\n  )\n  if (res$passed) passed <- passed + 1\n  # Save exponential samples\n  if (!is.null(res$samples)) {\n    writeLines(as.character(res$samples), \"/app/exponential_samples.txt\")\n  }\n\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  # Test 4: Gamma(5, 1) — shape > 1, log-concave\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  total <- total + 1\n  res <- run_distribution_test(\n    test_name = \"Gamma_shape5_rate1\",\n    f = function(x) dgamma(x, shape = 5, rate = 1),\n    lb = 0, ub = Inf,\n    ref_cdf = function(x) pgamma(x, shape = 5, rate = 1),\n    true_mean = 5, true_sd = sqrt(5),\n    x_init = c(1, 3, 5, 8)\n  )\n  if (res$passed) passed <- passed + 1\n\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  # Test 5: Beta(2, 5) on (0, 1)\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  total <- total + 1\n  res <- run_distribution_test(\n    test_name = \"Beta_2_5\",\n    f = function(x) dbeta(x, 2, 5),\n    lb = 0, ub = 1,\n    ref_cdf = function(x) pbeta(x, 2, 5),\n    true_mean = 2 / 7, true_sd = sqrt(10 / (49 * 8)),\n    x_init = c(0.1, 0.3, 0.6)\n  )\n  if (res$passed) passed <- passed + 1\n\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  # Test 6: Truncated Normal on (-2, 2)\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  total <- total + 1\n  # Truncated N(0,1) on (-2,2): mean=0, sd ~ 0.72\n  Z <- pnorm(2) - pnorm(-2)\n  trunc_sd <- sqrt(1 - 2 * 2 * dnorm(2) / Z)\n  res <- run_distribution_test(\n    test_name = \"Truncated_Normal_-2_2\",\n    f = dnorm,\n    lb = -2, ub = 2,\n    ref_cdf = function(x) {\n      (pnorm(pmin(pmax(x, -2), 2)) - pnorm(-2)) / Z\n    },\n    true_mean = 0, true_sd = trunc_sd,\n    x_init = c(-1, 0, 1)\n  )\n  if (res$passed) passed <- passed + 1\n\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  # Test 7: Logistic distribution\n  # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  total <- total + 1\n  res <- run_distribution_test(\n    test_name = \"Logistic_0_1\",\n    f = dlogis,\n    lb = -Inf, ub = Inf,\n    ref_cdf = plogis,\n    true_mean = 0, true_sd = pi / sqrt(3)\n  )\n  if (res$passed) passed <- passed + 1\n\n  # ==================================================================\n  # Input Validation Tests\n  # ==================================================================\n  cat(\"--- Input Validation Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  # Test: negative n\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Negative_n_rejected\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    ars(n = -5, f = dnorm)\n    cat(\"Negative_n_rejected: FAIL (no error raised)\\n\\n\")\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Caught: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Negative_n_rejected: PASS\\n\\n\")\n    passed <<- passed + 1\n  })\n\n  # Test: non-function f\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Non_function_f_rejected\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    ars(n = 100, f = \"not_a_function\")\n    cat(\"Non_function_f_rejected: FAIL (no error raised)\\n\\n\")\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Caught: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Non_function_f_rejected: PASS\\n\\n\")\n    passed <<- passed + 1\n  })\n\n  # Test: lb >= ub\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Invalid_domain_rejected\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    ars(n = 100, f = dnorm, lb = 5, ub = 2)\n    cat(\"Invalid_domain_rejected: FAIL (no error raised)\\n\\n\")\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Caught: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Invalid_domain_rejected: PASS\\n\\n\")\n    passed <<- passed + 1\n  })\n\n  # Test: non-log-concave density (Cauchy)\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Non_log_concave_rejected\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    ars(n = 100, f = dcauchy, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf, x_init = c(-5, -1, 0, 1, 5))\n    cat(\"Non_log_concave_rejected: FAIL (no error raised)\\n\\n\")\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Caught: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Non_log_concave_rejected: PASS\\n\\n\")\n    passed <<- passed + 1\n  })\n\n  # Test: n = 0\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Zero_n_rejected\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    ars(n = 0, f = dnorm)\n    cat(\"Zero_n_rejected: FAIL (no error raised)\\n\\n\")\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Caught: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Zero_n_rejected: PASS\\n\\n\")\n    passed <<- passed + 1\n  })\n\n  # Test: x_init outside domain\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Init_outside_domain_rejected\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    ars(n = 100, f = dnorm, lb = 0, ub = 10, x_init = c(-1, 5))\n    cat(\"Init_outside_domain_rejected: FAIL (no error raised)\\n\\n\")\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Caught: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Init_outside_domain_rejected: PASS\\n\\n\")\n    passed <<- passed + 1\n  })\n\n  # ==================================================================\n  # Module Tests\n  # ==================================================================\n  cat(\"--- Module Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  # Test: upper/lower hull evaluation\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Hull_evaluation\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    x_test <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_test)\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(x_test, h_test, -Inf, Inf)\n\n    # Upper hull must be >= log-density at all abscissae\n    for (xi in seq(-3, 3, by = 0.5)) {\n      u_val <- eval_upper_hull(xi, hull, x_test, h_test)\n      h_val <- eval_log_density(dnorm, xi)\n      if (u_val < h_val - 1e-6) {\n        stop(sprintf(\"Upper hull below log-density at x=%.2f: u=%.4f < h=%.4f\", xi, u_val, h_val))\n      }\n    }\n\n    # Lower hull must be <= log-density at interior points\n    for (xi in seq(-1.5, 1.5, by = 0.5)) {\n      l_val <- eval_lower_hull(xi, x_test, h_test)\n      h_val <- eval_log_density(dnorm, xi)\n      if (l_val > h_val + 1e-6) {\n        stop(sprintf(\"Lower hull above log-density at x=%.2f: l=%.4f > h=%.4f\", xi, l_val, h_val))\n      }\n    }\n\n    cat(\"  Upper hull >= log-density: OK\\n\")\n    cat(\"  Lower hull <= log-density: OK\\n\")\n    cat(\"Hull_evaluation: PASS\\n\\n\")\n    passed <<- passed + 1\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Hull_evaluation: FAIL\\n\\n\")\n  })\n\n  # Test: log-concavity detection\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Log_concavity_detection\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    # Non-log-concave: bimodal mixture\n    x_bimodal <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n    h_bimodal <- log(0.5 * dnorm(x_bimodal, -2) + 0.5 * dnorm(x_bimodal, 2))\n\n    err_caught <- FALSE\n    tryCatch({\n      check_log_concavity(x_bimodal, h_bimodal)\n    }, error = function(e) {\n      err_caught <<- TRUE\n    })\n\n    if (err_caught) {\n      cat(\"  Bimodal density correctly rejected\\n\")\n      cat(\"Log_concavity_detection: PASS\\n\\n\")\n      passed <<- passed + 1\n    } else {\n      cat(\"  Bimodal density was NOT rejected\\n\")\n      cat(\"Log_concavity_detection: FAIL\\n\\n\")\n    }\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Log_concavity_detection: FAIL\\n\\n\")\n  })\n\n  # Test: update_abscissae maintains sorted order\n  total <- total + 1\n  cat(\"Testing: Update_abscissae_sorted\\n\")\n  tryCatch({\n    x0 <- c(1, 3, 5, 7)\n    h0 <- c(-1, -0.5, -0.5, -1)\n    upd <- update_abscissae(4, -0.6, x0, h0)\n    if (all(diff(upd$x) > 0) && length(upd$x) == 5 && upd$x[3] == 4) {\n      cat(\"  Sorted order maintained after insertion\\n\")\n      cat(\"Update_abscissae_sorted: PASS\\n\\n\")\n      passed <<- passed + 1\n    } else {\n      cat(\"Update_abscissae_sorted: FAIL\\n\\n\")\n    }\n  }, error = function(e) {\n    cat(sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", e$message))\n    cat(\"Update_abscissae_sorted: FAIL\\n\\n\")\n  })\n\n  # ==================================================================\n  # Summary\n  # ==================================================================\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n  cat(sprintf(\"  Results: %d / %d tests passed\\n\", passed, total))\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n\n  invisible(passed == total)\n}\n\n\n# Run tests if this script is executed directly\nif (!interactive() && identical(sys.frame(1)$ofile, NULL)) {\n  # Only auto-run if sourced with --args test or run directly\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}
